macro_segments.title: Introduction to Epikoros: Definition and Preliminary Distinctions

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks on Perek Chelek and the concept of Epikoros
Epikoros as one who has no share in the world to come; etymology from Hefker
Three-tier definition: against God, against Torah, against Israel; relationship to Mevazeh
Mevazeh Talmid Chacham versus Mevazeh Chaveiro: which is more severe

macro_segments.title: Examples and Refinements of Epikoros: From Rabbi Yosef through Rav Pappa

micro_segments.title: First example: those who deny the benefit of the Rabbis and their teachings
Abaye's response: claiming Rabbis do nothing for us is Megale Panim BaTorah
Rav Nachman bar Yitzchak's supporting pasuk: Divine forgiveness based on merit of righteous
Discussion of Talmidei Chachamim as glue of the world; their role in maintaining covenant
Second example: student who attributes teaching to 'we' rather than to named rabbi
Rava's example: members of Binyamin's house questioning utility of Rabbis
Rava's test: attempting to permit raven and forbid dove to prove Rabbis' usefulness
Rav Pappa's example: casual, dismissive reference to 'those Rabbis' without specific attribution
Rav Pappa's remorse: sitting in a fast due to severity of offense

macro_segments.title: Incident of Levi bar Shmuel and Rav Huna bar Chiya: Disrespecting Megillat Esther

micro_segments.title: Levi bar Shmuel and Rav Huna bar Chiya wrapping Torah scrolls in Rav Yehuda's academy
Their refusal to wrap Megillat Esther, claiming it needs no scarf
Rav Yehuda's rebuke: disrespect toward Megillat Esther resembles Apikorsut

macro_segments.title: Respect for Teachers: Calling Rabbi by Name and the Case of Geichazi

micro_segments.title: Rav Nachman: one without share in world to come is one who calls his rabbi by his name
Rabbi Yochanan on Geichazi: punishment for calling teacher by name alone
Comparison with Eldad and Meidad and their address to Moshe

macro_segments.title: The Future Redemption: Stream from Holy of Holies and Precious Stones

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yirmiyah and Rabbi Zeira's teaching on the future stream with delicacies
Citation of Yechezkel's pasuk on fruit trees and medicinal leaves
Old man's interjection and Rabbi Yirmiyah's suspicion of Apikorsut
Rabbi Zeira's defense: old man was offering support, not mockery

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan's Teaching on Precious Stones and the Mocking Student

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Yochanan teaching on future gems and stones in Jerusalem's gates
Student's mockery: skepticism about existence of stones that large
Student's vision at sea: ministering angels sawing precious stones
Student returns to Rabbi Yochanan; is rebuked for not believing tradition without proof
Rabbi Yochanan's punishment: turning student to pile of bones

macro_segments.title: Textual Analysis of Yechezkel's Pasuk: Window Measurements and Medicinal Leaves

micro_segments.title: Gemara challenges measurements of precious stones; thirty by thirty only refers to windows
Analysis of 'Komemiyut' in Vayikra: heights of future redemption
Rabbi Meir vs. Rabbi Yehuda: will humans be two hundred or one hundred cubits tall
Meaning of 'V'aleihu Litrufa': medicinal leaves and competing interpretations
Wordplay on Trufa: opening the mouth of above and mouth of below
Rabbi Yochanan: Litrufa means literally medicine; Rabbi Shmuel: beautifying speakers

macro_segments.title: Reward and Merit in This World and the World to Come

micro_segments.title: Rav Yehuda bar Rav Simeon: blackening face in Torah study leads to brightness in world to come
Rav Tanchum bar Rav Chanilai: starving oneself for Torah leads to satiation in world to come
Rav Dimi: Holy One will give each righteous person His full measure
Abaye's challenge: is it possible that God gives His entire load to a person
Rav Yehoshua's counter-challenge: how can we say measure for measure when heavens are measured in finger

macro_segments.title: Measure for Measure: Divine Measure of Goodness and Punishment

micro_segments.title: Gemara distinguishes measure of goodness from measure of punishment
Measure of goodness: doors of heaven opened, raining food; measure of punishment: only chimneys open
Challenge: fire in this world immediately burns; in world to come it keeps burning forever
Resolution: God gives strength to wicked to accept punishment and to righteous to receive good

macro_segments.title: External Books (Sefarim Chitzoniyim): Ben Sira and the Sadducean Writings

micro_segments.title: Rabbi Akiva: one who reads in external books has no share in world to come
Introduction: external books include Ben Sira and Sadducean writings
Significance of preposition 'B' (in): immersion in external books versus mere knowledge of content
Rabbis' concern: protecting boundary between Jewish tradition and emerging Christian texts

macro_segments.title: Examples from Ben Sira: Defending Against the Prohibition

micro_segments.title: First example: instruction on skinning fish; Rabbis teach similar principle of waste
Second example: daughters as false treasures; Rabbis also acknowledge gender necessity
Third example: pain in heart kills mighty man; Shlomo says the same with wordplay on crushing worry
Rav Ammi and Rav Assi: two interpretations of how to handle worry—push away or discuss with others
